support.why_not_vp: No CEO-irreplaceable lever: no blocked discount awaiting approval, no roadmap commitment demanded, no SE/POC guarantee needed, and the unengaged economic buyers (CIO John Hill / Akumark) can be reached via champion Attila or the RSD. Everything outstanding (champion development, ROI/TCO model, exec intro request) can be driven by the AE/RVP/CRO.

monitor.reason: Our own demo slide deck to the buyer is overdue on a $320k deal heading into an imminent early-July shortlist cut with no champion secured.

triggers.summary: Zycus demo deck to Reema/Attila overdue as shortlist cut looms
triggers.detail: On the $320k Avaya S2P deal (Formal Evaluation), our committed deliverable to 'share the demo slide deck and coordinate customer references' was due Jun 26 and is still marked overdue. This is our lapse, not buyer-dependent, and it lands exactly when the buyer is finishing its RFI scoring and shortlist cut in early July. Zycus already entered 'last minute' with catch-up to do, has no confirmed champion (Attila still only developing), and the economic buyers (CIO John Hill, Akumark) are unengaged - a missed deliverable now signals slippage right before the cut and risks Zycus dropping off the shortlist.
triggers.metric: Demo deck deliverable overdue 7 days (due Jun 26, still open as of Jul 3)
triggers.ceo_ask: Ask Edward why the demo slide deck and reference intros (due Jun 26) to Reema and Attila are still overdue on a $320k deal, and whether we've locked Attila as champion before the early-July shortlist cut.
triggers.evidence: our_open_deliverables: 'Share demo slide deck and coordinate customer references if helpful' due 2026-06-26, status 'overdue' (item dated 2026-06-19); our own 2026-06-30 follow-up email went out without closing it, so this is our lapse, not buyer-dependent.
